text:
KINGS NEUROSCIENCES CENTRE

Clinic Date: 30 January 2018

Dr Wang
Saffron Park Hospital

Saffron Park, London, E14 7JL
Dear Dr Wang
John Doe, DOB: 21-11-1982, Hospital No: P546484 NHS No. 5484656746
Flat 15 Roundwood Road, London, E14 7JL

Thank you for referring your patient, whom I reviewed today in the Neurology Clinic. Since I last saw him, he reports a clear deterioration in overall stability, which he attributes to greater social pressures and a higher level of alcohol exposure lately. He described increased tiredness, fragmented sleep, and heightened anxiety around work and family commitments. He has not noted any new focal neurological symptoms between episodes, but he and his partner report more prolonged post-event confusion and low mood afterwards.

He explained that since discharge from hospital he has experienced two to four seizures, the last one being on 29 November. These events were witnessed on two occasions by his partner and once at work; recovery was slower than previously reported. He has kept a diary but acknowledges missed entries around social events where alcohol was involved. There have been no injuries, though he described tongue soreness and diffuse myalgia after the last episode. He denies any clear precipitant other than poor sleep and alcohol on the weekends preceding events.

Current treatment consists of Levetiracetam 1250 mg twice daily, Topiramate 125 mg twice daily, Sodium Valproate 500 mg twice daily, Clobazam 10 mg twice daily, and Carbamazepine 400 mg twice daily. He remains generally adherent, though he admits to two missed evening doses in December following late nights. He reports daytime somnolence and mild cognitive slowing, which may be compounded by stress and alcohol. No rash, but he notes intermittent pruritus of the lower legs; there are healed scratch marks without signs of infection.

On examination today, observations were stable and neurological examination was non-focal. There were no signs of acute systemic illness. Given the recent deterioration in control in the context of increased alcohol intake and psychosocial strain, we discussed harm minimisation, adherence, and sleep hygiene. He is motivated to reduce alcohol and has agreed to keep a structured event and trigger diary. I have requested updated bloods (FBC, U&E, LFTs) to assess for any treatment-related factors that could contribute to fatigue or pruritus, and I have asked him to attend for a routine drug review clinic to reassess tolerability. We also discussed referral to the epilepsy specialist nurse for counselling support around lifestyle and stress management.

At this stage, in the absence of clear new localising features and pending corroborative information from his diary and blood tests, I have not altered his medication today. However, in view of the deterioration and the last event being relatively recent, I have brought forward his follow-up to 8–10 weeks, with instructions to contact the service promptly should there be any further events or concerns. I will update you once results are available and after review with the specialist nurse team.

Thank you for your ongoing support with reinforcing alcohol reduction, consistent medication adherence, and sleep regularity. Please let me know if there are any issues in the interim.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: he has experienced two to four seizures, the last one being on 29 November
He explained that since discharge from hospital he has experienced two to four seizures, the last one being on 29 November

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "he has experienced two to four seizures, the last one being on 29 November." There is no time window specified for these 2–4 seizures other than "since discharge from hospital," which is undefined. Without a defined period (day/week/month/year), we cannot compute a rate. The patient is not seizure free (last event recent), so seizure-free labeling is not applicable. Therefore, per instruction, if seizures are mentioned but specific frequency cannot be determined, return "unknown."
seizure_frequency_number: he has experienced two to four seizures, the last one being on 29 November
